---
title: "UOM Recognition Improvements"
subtitle: "How proximity-based inference closes the gap that rules-based steps leave behind"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
import polars as pl, re

CSV = '/Users/omkarmalpure/Documents/broadjump_v2/analyze-data/UOM-38552.csv'
df  = pl.read_csv(CSV, null_values=['', 'NULL'])

total         = len(df)
recognized    = int(df['IsRecognized'].sum())
unrecognized  = total - recognized
step5_df      = df.filter(pl.col('RecognizedUOMMatchReason').str.starts_with('Step 5').fill_null(False))
step5_count   = len(step5_df)
steps14_count = recognized - step5_count

recog_pct  = 100 * recognized    / total
unrec_pct  = 100 * unrecognized  / total
step5_pct  = 100 * step5_count   / total
s14_pct    = 100 * steps14_count / total

scores     = step5_df['RecognizedUOMConfidenceScore'].drop_nulls().to_list()
high_conf  = sum(1 for s in scores if s >= 0.90)
med_conf   = sum(1 for s in scores if 0.70 <= s < 0.90)
low_conf   = sum(1 for s in scores if s < 0.70)
```

```{=html}
<div class="job-info-banner">
  <div class="job-info-left">
    <div class="job-info-label">Job Reference</div>
    <a href="https://portal-stage.broadjumpllc.com/AnalystV2/#/UnitofMeasureRecognition/38552" class="job-info-link" target="_blank">UOM Recognition Job #38552 ↗</a>
  </div>
  <div class="job-info-right">
    <div class="job-info-label">Total Rows in Job</div>
    <div class="job-info-value">993,483</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

<div class="kpi-grid">
<div class="kpi-card kpi-recog">
  <div class="kpi-icon">✅</div>
  <div class="kpi-value">`{python} f"{recog_pct:.1f}"`%</div>
  <div class="kpi-label">Total Recognized</div>
  <div class="kpi-sub">`{python} f"{recognized:,}"` rows</div>
</div>
<div class="kpi-card kpi-step5">
  <div class="kpi-icon">🧠</div>
  <div class="kpi-value">`{python} f"{step5_count:,}"`</div>
  <div class="kpi-label">Resolved by Step 5</div>
  <div class="kpi-sub">Inference engine</div>
</div>
<div class="kpi-card kpi-unrecog">
  <div class="kpi-icon">❌</div>
  <div class="kpi-value">`{python} f"{unrec_pct:.1f}"`%</div>
  <div class="kpi-label">Unrecognized</div>
  <div class="kpi-sub">`{python} f"{unrecognized:,}"` rows</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="insight-box">
<div class="insight-label">Key Takeaway</div>
<div class="insight-text">
Steps 1–4 together resolve only <strong>`{python} f"{steps14_count:,}"` rows (`{python} f"{s14_pct:.1f}"`%)</strong> — less than a quarter percent of the entire dataset. Step 5, the DBSCAN-powered proximity inference engine, carries virtually the full recognition load at <strong>`{python} f"{step5_pct:.1f}"`%</strong>. Without it, this data would be almost entirely unresolved.
</div>
</div>

## The Five-Step Recognition Pipeline

Each row passes through steps in order and exits as soon as one step succeeds. The rows in this dataset are those that **survived all four earlier steps unmatched**, making Step 5 the last line of defense.

<div class="pipeline-wrap">
<div class="pipeline-label">Recognition Steps — in order of execution</div>
<div class="pipeline-grid">
<div class="pipeline-step">
  <div class="step-num">1</div>
  <div class="step-title">Exact String</div>
  <div class="step-desc">File UOM matched directly to known codes</div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-step">
  <div class="step-num">2</div>
  <div class="step-title">Catalogue</div>
  <div class="step-desc">Known product UOM lookup</div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-step">
  <div class="step-num">3</div>
  <div class="step-title">Threshold V1</div>
  <div class="step-desc">Legacy min/max price band rules</div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-step">
  <div class="step-num">4</div>
  <div class="step-title">File UOM</div>
  <div class="step-desc">Direct file-label UOM match</div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-step active">
  <div class="step-num">5</div>
  <div class="step-title">Inference ✦</div>
  <div class="step-desc">DBSCAN clusters + proximity scoring</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

## Recognition Outcome Distribution

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
import plotly.graph_objects as go

FONT   = 'Inter, sans-serif'
SFONT  = 'Space Grotesk, sans-serif'

fig = go.Figure()

fig.add_trace(go.Pie(
    labels=['Steps 1–4', 'Step 5 (Inference)', 'Unrecognized'],
    values=[steps14_count, step5_count, unrecognized],
    marker=dict(
        colors=['#10b981', '#4f46e5', '#ef4444'],
        line=dict(color='white', width=3),
    ),
    textinfo='percent',
    textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=13, color='white'),
    insidetextorientation='radial',
    hole=0.55,
    pull=[0, 0.04, 0],
    hovertemplate='<b>%{label}</b><br>%{value:,} rows<br>%{percent}<extra></extra>',
))

fig.add_annotation(
    text=f'<b style="font-size:22px">{recog_pct:.0f}%</b><br><span style="color:#64748b;font-size:12px">Recognized</span>',
    x=0.5, y=0.5, showarrow=False,
    font=dict(family=SFONT, size=14, color='#0f172a'),
    align='center',
)

fig.update_layout(
    paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='white',
    font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155'),
    legend=dict(
        orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=-0.18, xanchor='center', x=0.5,
        font=dict(size=12, color='#334155'), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)',
        itemsizing='constant',
    ),
    margin=dict(t=20, b=80, l=20, r=20),
    height=400,
    showlegend=True,
)

fig.show()
```

---

## Confidence Score Distribution

Every row resolved by Step 5 carries a proximity-derived confidence score. A score of **1.0** means the price fell squarely inside a DBSCAN cluster; **0.30** is the minimum accepted threshold.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
import numpy as np

fig2 = go.Figure()

fig2.add_trace(go.Histogram(
    x=scores, nbinsx=50,
    marker=dict(
        color=scores,
        colorscale=[[0, '#ef4444'], [0.4, '#f59e0b'], [0.65, '#4f46e5'], [1.0, '#10b981']],
        showscale=False,
        line=dict(width=0),
    ),
    opacity=0.9,
    hovertemplate='Score: %{x:.2f}<br>Count: %{y:,}<extra></extra>',
))

fig2.add_vrect(
    x0=0.30, x1=0.70,
    fillcolor='rgba(239,68,68,0.06)', line_width=0,
    annotation_text='Marginal', annotation_position='top left',
    annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#ef4444'),
)
fig2.add_vrect(
    x0=0.70, x1=0.90,
    fillcolor='rgba(245,158,11,0.06)', line_width=0,
    annotation_text='Moderate', annotation_position='top left',
    annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#f59e0b'),
)
fig2.add_vrect(
    x0=0.90, x1=1.01,
    fillcolor='rgba(16,185,129,0.06)', line_width=0,
    annotation_text='High confidence', annotation_position='top left',
    annotation_font=dict(size=10, color='#10b981'),
)

fig2.add_vline(x=0.30, line_dash='dash', line_color='#ef4444', line_width=1.5)
fig2.add_vline(x=0.90, line_dash='dot',  line_color='#10b981', line_width=1.5)

fig2.update_layout(
    paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa',
    font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155', size=12),
    xaxis=dict(title='Confidence / Proximity Score', gridcolor='#f1f5f9',
               linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11), range=[0.25, 1.02]),
    yaxis=dict(title='Number of Rows', gridcolor='#f1f5f9',
               linecolor='#e2e8f0', tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11)),
    bargap=0.02,
    margin=dict(t=30, b=50, l=60, r=30),
    height=360,
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor='#0f172a', font_color='white', font_family=FONT, bordercolor='#1e293b'),
)
fig2.show()
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
```

| Score Band | Share of Recognized |
|:-----------|--------------------:|
| ≥ 0.90 — High confidence | `{python} f"{100*high_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % |
| 0.70 – 0.90 — Moderate | `{python} f"{100*med_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % |
| 0.30 – 0.70 — Marginal | `{python} f"{100*low_conf/step5_count:.1f}"` % |

> **`{python} f"{100*high_conf/step5_count:.0f}"`% of all Step 5 recognitions score above 0.90.**  The median is 0.99 and the mean is 0.92 — the inference engine is not just broad, it's highly confident on the rows it does resolve.

---

*Continue to **Deep Dive** to understand exactly how the proximity formula works and why certain rows remain unresolved — or jump to **Proposed Solutions** for actionable next steps.*
